items.headline: Major elevator upgrade underway at Ridder Park commercial hub
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project is launching at 1290 Ridder Park Dr, suggesting significant investment in San José's commercial infrastructure. This type of major building system upgrade typically indicates a larger commercial or office complex addressing aging infrastructure.

items.headline: City faces lawsuit over Mary Avenue safety from Garden Gate Coalition
items.summary: Cupertino is defending against litigation brought by the Garden Gate Coalition regarding Mary Avenue safety concerns. The case is pending in Santa Clara Superior Court, suggesting residents have organized around a specific traffic or pedestrian safety issue on that street.

items.headline: Commercial/industrial development begins on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial project has been permitted at 1300 W San Carlos St. This represents new commercial activity in San José's industrial corridor, though permit details don't specify the business type.

items.headline: Gas utility rates being adjusted for 2027
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending residential and commercial gas service rate schedules and approving a new financial forecast for the gas utility. Rate changes take effect in Fiscal Year 2027.

items.headline: City launches business amnesty program and retail incentives
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out a Business License Amnesty Program targeting shopping centers, plus new outreach and incentive programs for retail and small businesses. This suggests the city is working to formalize unlicensed businesses and boost the local commercial sector.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes annual San José sponsored event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event and authorized city funding and donations for the cleanup initiative. This formalizes community participation in local environmental cleanup efforts.

items.headline: Community development grant funding applications being reviewed
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission will hear presentations from applicants seeking Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These grants support local housing and community development projects.

items.headline: New retail rules coming to Palo Alto neighborhoods
items.summary: The City Council is considering changes to zoning and building codes to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. This could affect where and how retail businesses operate throughout Palo Alto.
